This kernel appears to be a synthetic/contrived microbenchmark rather than something from a well-known codebase or tutorial. The specific characteristics — the magic constants (17, 4095, 257, 8 iterations, 1.1f multiplier), the bitwise AND masking for circular buffer-style access, and the particular access pattern — don't match any standard benchmark suite (Rodinia, Parboil, SHOC, etc.) or well-known CUDA tutorial that I'm aware of. It looks like a hand-crafted test kernel, possibly designed to study memory access patterns, cache behavior, or instruction-level parallelism.
